denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 02/16/2026
Aetna Choice® POS II (employer-sponsored group health plan)
State of issue: Illinois          Plan type: Fully-Insured
Member ID: W100190056          Group #: GRP-1024
Authorization / Reference #: UM-2026-107309
Claim #: CLM-2026-189273
Date of Service (requested): 01/27/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Central States Medical Associates

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's precertification / medical necessity review request on 01/29/2026 for MRI lumbar spine without contrast related to Lumbar disc herniation, suspected (M51.26). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: MRI lumbar spine without contrast
Diagnosis (as submitted): Lumbar disc herniation, suspected (M51.26)
Denial code: CO-96 — Non-covered charge — service denied as not medically necessary under plan criteria.
Clinical policy applied: CPB 0124 under Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin (CPB) and InterQual® acute-care criteria

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
This determination is based on a plan exclusion. Notwithstanding the Illinois mandated benefit requirement under 215 ILCS 5/356z et seq., which requires coverage of diagnostic imaging for musculoskeletal conditions for fully-insured plans issued in Illinois, this determination is governed by the terms of your employer's group health plan document, and state coverage mandates do not alter the terms of this plan for this determination.
Our review applied the criteria in CPB 0124 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Aetna medical policy requires documented failure of prerequisite therapies before MRI lumbar spine without contrast. Submitted records do not establish failure of required lower-cost options for Lumbar disc herniation, suspected (M51.26).

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with an Aetna medical director to review clinical information before you file a written appeal.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file, including documents, clinical criteria, and other information we considered in making this determination. Submit your request to the address shown on this notice or through the member portal identified in your plan materials.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W100190056) and reference UM-2026-107309.

This notice does not include information about your right to bring a civil action under Section 502(a) of ERISA.

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

Aetna Medical Management
Utilization Management Department
clinical_context: The 27-year-old member has Lumbar disc herniation, suspected (M51.26). She has been treated under a fully-insured commercial plan issued in Illinois, with MRI lumbar spine without contrast recommended as the next appropriate step. Radicular pain 6 weeks; failed PT; straight-leg raise positive.
